Explanation: This report analyzes chromatographic peak shapes using Tailing Factor (TF) and Fronting Factor (FF) metrics. While the core objective is clearly stated, execution has notable weaknesses. The methodology section is difficult to follow due to incomplete sentences and poorly formatted mathematical expressions. The figures (Figures 1-2) appear uninformative—showing mostly empty space with minimal data visualization. The authors acknowledge limitations due to missing metadata but do not adequately compensate through alternative analysis approaches. A significant contradiction exists: while visual inspection flags several runs exceeding the USP ¡621¿ threshold (TF/FF > 2.0), statistical Z-score analysis finds zero outliers, but this discrepancy is inadequately explained. The code provided reveals errors (e.g., Line 2 has a bug using df_2['TF_flag'] instead of df_2['FF_flag']), suggesting implementation issues. The analysis lacks depth—no investigation into root causes, limited discussion of column health factors, and no cross-stage analysis despite the availability of 16 purification stages. The writing is generally acceptable but includes formatting issues. The report would benefit from deeper investigation, clearer visualizations, and resolution of the statistical contradictions.
